data.title: Sinopharm Subsidiary Posts 2025 Revenue Growth Amid Flat Earnings and Stronger Cash Flow
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sinopharm Group Co ( (HK:1099) ) has issued an update.

Sinopharm Group has disclosed the 2025 audited financial results of its Shanghai-listed subsidiary China National Medicines Corporation, showing modest top-line growth but largely flat profitability. Revenue rose 3.7% year on year to RMB 52.47 billion, while net profit attributable to shareholders slipped 0.18% to RMB 1.997 billion, and earnings per share were essentially unchanged.

Despite the slight earnings decline, SINOPHARM CNCM improved its cash generation, with net operating cash flow up 20.88% versus 2024 and net assets attributable to shareholders increasing 8.27%. The weighted average return on net assets fell by nearly one percentage point, indicating some margin pressure even as total assets expanded 5.03%, suggesting steady but moderating profitability for stakeholders in a growing asset base.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1099) stock is a Buy with a HK$23.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sinopharm Group Co stock, see the HK:1099 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sinopharm Group Co

Sinopharm Group Co. is a major Chinese pharmaceutical and healthcare company, with its subsidiary China National Medicines Corporation Ltd. (SINOPHARM CNCM) engaged in the distribution and trade of medicines and related healthcare products. The subsidiary’s A shares are listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange, positioning it as a significant player in China’s pharmaceutical supply chain.

Average Trading Volume: 5,305,954

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$65.72B

Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6693) ) has provided an announcement.

Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co., Ltd. has announced it will hold an online briefing on its 2025 annual results and proposed cash dividend distribution from 4:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m. Hong Kong time on March 26, 2026, via the Shanghai Stock Exchange Roadshow Center. Senior management and independent non-executive directors will attend to discuss operating performance, key financial indicators and the profit distribution plan, with investors invited to submit questions in advance through the roadshow website, underscoring the company’s emphasis on disclosure and investor engagement.

The event is positioned as an interactive platform where the company will address issues of general concern to shareholders within the bounds of disclosure rules, potentially offering greater clarity on its dividend policy and capital allocation for 2025. By organizing this voluntary briefing and facilitating pre-submitted questions, Chifeng Jilong aims to strengthen market confidence, improve transparency around its financial strategy and maintain active dialogue with both institutional and retail investors.

data.title: Asiasec Properties Swings to Deeper Loss on Investment Property Revaluations
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Asiasec Properties Limited ( (HK:0271) ).

Asiasec Properties Limited, a Hong Kong-based property company, generates revenue mainly from rental income, building management fees and associated property services tied to its investment property portfolio. The group’s operations are driven by the performance and fair value of its property assets, alongside ongoing cost management in areas such as staff, repairs and maintenance.

For the year ended 31 December 2025, Asiasec reported revenue growth to HK$50.3 million but saw its net loss widen sharply to HK$214.2 million, largely due to a substantial HK$206.7 million loss from the change in fair value of its investment properties. Despite lower finance costs and an income tax credit, higher repairs, maintenance and other operating expenses, combined with negative fair value movements, significantly deteriorated overall profitability and increased the basic loss per share to 17.26 Hong Kong cents, underscoring continued pressure on asset valuations and returns for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0271) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.18 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Asiasec Properties Limited stock, see the HK:0271 Stock Forecast page.

More about Asiasec Properties Limited

Asiasec Properties Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company engaged in property-related activities, deriving income primarily from rental operations and associated property services. The group focuses on managing investment properties, earning revenue from rents, building management fees and related ancillary services within the Hong Kong property market.

data.title: APAC Resources Finalises Re-Domiciliation to Hong Kong and Shifts Registered Office
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from APAC Resources ( (HK:1104) ) is now available.

APAC Resources Limited has completed its re-domiciliation from Bermuda to Hong Kong, with the change of domicile, adoption of new articles of association and a Chinese name, and the elimination and application of contributed surplus all becoming effective on 19 March 2026. The company emphasised that the move does not create a new legal entity or disrupt business continuity but is intended to strengthen its strategic presence in Hong Kong and bolster investor confidence, while its Hong Kong share registrar remains unchanged and its registered office has been formally shifted to Wanchai.

Following the re-domicile, APAC Resources will proceed to apply for deregistration in Bermuda, signalling a full transition of its corporate home to Hong Kong’s jurisdiction. The board also cautioned shareholders, warrantholders and potential investors to exercise care when dealing in the company’s securities during this period of structural change, underscoring the ongoing nature of the implementation process and associated regulatory steps.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1104) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on APAC Resources stock, see the HK:1104 Stock Forecast page.

More about APAC Resources

APAC Resources Limited is a Hong Kong-listed investment and resource-focused company that has historically operated from Bermuda but maintains its primary presence and investor base in Hong Kong. The group is positioned within the broader resources and investment sector and is aligning its corporate structure more closely with the Hong Kong market and regulatory environment.

Giant Biogene Holding Co. Ltd. ( (HK:2367) ) has issued an announcement.

Giant Biogene Holding Co., Ltd. has declared a special cash dividend of RMB 0.6714 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, underscoring management’s willingness to return excess capital to shareholders. The dividend, subject to shareholder approval on 28 May 2026, will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 25 June 2026, with the ex-dividend date set for 1 June and the record date for 8 June, giving investors clear timelines to position around the payout.

The move signals confidence in the company’s cash generation and balance sheet strength, and may enhance its appeal to income-focused investors in the biotech space. By opting for a special dividend rather than a permanent increase in its regular payout, the company preserves financial flexibility while rewarding existing shareholders, which could support its valuation and trading liquidity around key dividend dates.

data.title: Infinity Logistics Delays 2025 Results and Faces Trading Suspension After Auditor Change
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited ( (HK:1442) ).

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited has warned that it will miss the 31 March 2026 deadline for publishing its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025, after the late appointment of new auditor Nexia in February created additional time pressure for completing key audit procedures. The group says it is prioritising completion of the audit over issuing unaudited management accounts to avoid confusing shareholders and expects to release preliminary 2025 results on 9 April and dispatch its annual report by 30 April, with a board meeting scheduled for 9 April to approve the accounts and consider a final dividend.

As a consequence of the delay, trading in the company’s shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange will be suspended from 1 April 2026 and will only resume once the 2025 results are published, a move that temporarily restricts liquidity and heightens caution for investors. The board says it is not aware of any other material audit issues beyond the timing impact of the auditor change, but has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise care in dealing in the company’s securities during the suspension period.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1442) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited stock, see the HK:1442 Stock Forecast page.

More about Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating in the logistics and transport sector through its group subsidiaries. The company provides logistics, freight and related transport services, positioning itself within regional and international supply chains for a broad base of customers.

data.title: Road King Flags Sharply Wider 2025 Loss on Prolonged Property Slump
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Road King Infrastructure ( (HK:1098) ) has provided an announcement.

Road King Infrastructure has warned that it expects to post a significantly wider net loss for the year ended 31 December 2025, projecting a group net loss of HK$5.2 billion to HK$5.5 billion and a loss attributable to shareholders of HK$5.8 billion to HK$6.1 billion. The deterioration from 2024 results is mainly driven by a prolonged slump in the property market and a harsh operating environment that have eroded margins on property projects in Mainland China and Hong Kong and forced higher impairment charges on properties and related assets.

The company noted that last year’s smaller loss was partly cushioned by a substantial one-off gain from the disposal of its entire toll road business in Mainland China, a benefit that will not recur in 2025, thereby exposing the full extent of operational weakness in its core property portfolio. The figures are based on unaudited management estimates, and the company has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution in trading its shares ahead of the formal annual results announcement expected in late March 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1098) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Road King Infrastructure stock, see the HK:1098 Stock Forecast page.

More about Road King Infrastructure

Road King Infrastructure Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda that operates through subsidiaries across Mainland China and Hong Kong. The group has historically been involved in infrastructure-related businesses, including toll roads, and currently has significant exposure to property development projects in these markets, leaving it sensitive to fluctuations in regional real estate conditions.

Stella International Holdings Limited ( (HK:1836) ) has issued an update.

Stella International Holdings reported a 3.8% rise in shipment volume for 2025, driven largely by its Sports footwear category, with revenue edging up 1.6% to US$1.57 billion. Despite higher cost of sales compressing gross profit, the group maintained an operating profit of US$149.2 million, equivalent to a 9.5% operating margin, underscoring resilient profitability in a competitive environment.

Net profit came in at US$137.0 million, supported by interest income and a modest contribution from a joint venture, while the company preserved a solid net cash position of US$367.4 million. Stella also acquired a high-end handbag factory in Vietnam to broaden its premium accessories offering and declared total dividends of HK145 cents per share, including a special payout, signaling confidence in its cash generation and commitment to shareholder returns.

Star Plus Legend Holdings Limited ( (HK:6683) ) has issued an announcement.

Star Plus Legend Holdings Limited has scheduled a board meeting for 31 March 2026 to review and approve the annual results for the financial year ended 31 December 2025. The board will also consider the recommendation of a final dividend, if any, and address other corporate matters, a step that will shape the company’s capital allocation decisions and inform shareholders about its performance and potential returns.

The announcement confirms the current composition of the board, including the chairperson, executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. This disclosure underscores the company’s compliance with Hong Kong listing governance requirements and provides transparency to investors regarding oversight of financial reporting and dividend policy.

More about Star Plus Legend Holdings Limited

Star Plus Legend Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 6683. China PengFei Group Limited ( (HK:3348) ) just unveiled an update.

China PengFei Group Limited has scheduled a board meeting for 31 March 2026 to review and approve the audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025, covering the Company and its subsidiaries. data.title: Kinetic Development Advances Yong’an Coal Mine Toward Full Production
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Kinetic Mines & Energy Ltd. ( (HK:1277) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Kinetic Development Group has invested about RMB4.6 billion from accumulated profits in acquiring Ningxia Kinetic Mining and constructing the Yong’an and Weiyi coal mines, including RMB3.122 billion for acquisition and about RMB1.5 billion for mine construction. The Yong’an Coal Mine holds around 224 million tonnes of coal resources, with designed annual capacity of 1.2 million tonnes using a dual-seam, fully mechanised underground mining model.

The Yong’an mine entered joint trial operations in November 2024, with the lower coal group systems running normally and development of the upper coal mining area nearing key milestones. The company plans to produce about 500,000 tonnes of raw coal from the lower seam in 2026 and ramp up to full designed capacity of 1.2 million tonnes in 2027, marking a transition from construction to formal production and potentially strengthening its position in the coking coal market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1277) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kinetic Mines & Energy Ltd. stock, see the HK:1277 Stock Forecast page.

More about Kinetic Mines & Energy Ltd.

Kinetic Development Group Limited is a coal mining and energy company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong. Through its subsidiary Ningxia Kinetic Mining in the Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region, the group focuses on developing underground coal mines producing fat coal and high-quality coking coal for coking, blending, power generation and coal liquefaction markets.
